preview_text:
The model favors Czech Republic with a 51% win probability, giving them a modest edge over South Africa at 19%. The margin reflects a mismatch in underlying strength between the two, though not a dominant forecast; the underdog retains a genuine chance.

The 29.6% draw probability sits between the win chances, suggesting a competitive match with plausible outcomes across all three results. The spread is neither tight nor wide—Czech Republic is favored but the fixture carries meaningful uncertainty, with nearly three in ten scenarios ending level.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned. Both assign Czech Republic a 46–51% win rate and South Africa roughly 19–24%, with draws near 30%. The largest divergence is a 4.7 percentage-point gap on South Africa's win probability, well within normal variance between forecast methods. No Polymarket data is yet available for this fixture.
